title: Exponent
short: The power a base is raised to: in b^n, the exponent is n.
full:
An exponent records repeated multiplication: b^n means b multiplied by itself n times. The laws of exponents mirror the laws of logarithms — multiplying powers adds exponents, raising a power to a power multiplies them.

An exponential function b^x (with base b > 0, b ≠ 1) grows or decays at a rate proportional to its own value, which is why it models compound interest, populations and radioactive decay. Its inverse is the logarithm.
